findings.claim: D5's 'select_mode close-efficacy from each element's own geo' has NO per-element position or per-element gap threaded to the coupling comparator or to the damage path — a genuine schema gap that makes 'close-efficacy derived from its own geo' un-computable without new plumbing the plan doesn't spec. The mode system is already internally split: affordance RANKING reads per-element baked geo, but the coupling COMPARISON and all downstream damage read the whole-weapon baked gap.
findings.evidence: systems.element_afforded (systems.py:272-283) reads geo=el['geo'] and thresholds geo['gap']/geo['cut'] PER ELEMENT (the mode_elements are baked per-element at weapons.py:745-747 into _me['geo']). But systems.select_mode's greedy comparator (systems.py:325-326) passes gap_prec=w['gap'] — the WHOLE-WEAPON baked gap (weapons.py:737) — uniformly for EVERY candidate head, NOT the element's geo['gap']. And core.strike (core.py:169-171) routes the SELECTED sel_head to damage() but passes attacker.w['gap'] (whole-weapon) and WP.percussion_authority(attacker.w) (whole-weapon) as the coupling operands. So today a poleaxe that selects its spike (sel_head='point') is scored/damaged on the whole-weapon w['gap']=0.78, not the spike element's own geo. mode_elements carry {head, geo} but no x_m position (contrast weapons.py elements[] which carry x_m). A 'close-efficacy factor derived from a swing element's own arc geo' needs (a) the element's geo threaded into the comparator instead of w['gap'], AND (b) some per-element positional/range notion to say a broad-arc element 'collapses in tight quarters' — neither exists. select_mode also currently receives `closed` and never reads it (wrapper.py:69 passes it; systems.py:302 signature ignores it), the exact dead param the plan flags in D5.
findings.recommendation: Before I4, define the schema: either (1) thread each candidate element's own geo['gap']/geo['cut'] into the coupling comparator (replacing the uniform w['gap']) AND add a per-element position/reach-profile field to mode_elements so 'arc-requiring swing collapses in tight quarters' is computable, or (2) explicitly scope D5 to whole-weapon close-efficacy only (no per-element measure gating) and document that mode-elements remain positionless. Do not ship a close-efficacy factor that claims to read 'its own geo' while the comparator and damage path still read w['gap'].

findings.claim: Half-baked mode WITHIN a single beat's damage resolution: if D2 makes heft(w, grip, range_avail) circumstance-aware (cut/thrust path), the SIBLING blunt path in the SAME damage() call still reads static whole-weapon percussion_authority(w). A composite that select_mode routes to a blunt element (mace, poleaxe hammer, lucerne/bec/goedendag blunt) will resolve its impact from a grip-INVARIANT percussion authority while a cut/thrust weapon in the mirror resolves from a grip-AWARE heft — an internally inconsistent 'choked-up thrust degrades but choked-up percussion does not' asymmetry inside one beat, contradicting D2's own falsifiable 'choked-up swing < full-extension swing'.
findings.evidence: core.damage (core.py:153): `heft = 3.0*(perc/8.0) if weapon_head=='blunt' else HEFT_HEAVY*heft_units`. The blunt branch reads `perc` = WP.percussion_authority(attacker.w) (core.py:171, static derive(w) via the cached _derived, weapons.py:756), the else branch reads heft_units = heft_resp = WP.heft(w) (core.py:69, also static derive(w) TODAY). D2 widens heft() to (w,grip,range_avail) but does NOT list percussion_authority as circumstance-aware in the plan's D1/D2 (D2 says 'percussion_authority and agility likewise read the bundle' as an aside, but the swing-branch KE reformulation and HEFT_REF re-anchor are cut/thrust-only). percussion_authority's swing authority is sqrt(mass)*pob_frac*energy_credit (weapon_physics.py:224) with NO grip term — a choked poleaxe hammer reads identical percussion to full extension. Because select_mode picks blunt-vs-point per beat, the two paths coexist across the roster in the same sweep.
findings.recommendation: Make percussion_authority circumstance-aware in the SAME increment as heft (I2b), not a later one — both feed core.damage's Impact term and must degrade coherently under grip/range. If percussion is deliberately left grip-invariant (a defensible physics choice — a hammer's mass doesn't move on the haft), state that explicitly and prove the blunt/cut asymmetry is intended, or the ablation gate will read the inconsistency as noise.

findings.claim: D3's 'wrapper's frozen er dict' is the correctly-identified freeze, but it is NOT the only per-engagement freeze the plan must unfreeze. The `longer`/`shorter` role labels and `measure_gap`/`closed` are ALSO computed ONCE from the frozen er before the beat loop, and the entire APPROACH phase (stop-hit, close_rate, reach_threat) keys off those frozen labels. Grip-aware reach that recomputes er per-beat but leaves longer/shorter frozen creates a geometry where the recomputed reaches can invert while the labels do not — an internally inconsistent reach state the plan's I3 acceptance ('polearm-vs-arming drops out of the band') may mask rather than fix.
findings.evidence: wrapper.py:32-38: erA/erB and er={A,B} computed once; `longer`/`shorter` set once (line 37); measure_gap and `closed` set once (line 38). The whole approach block (wrapper.py:85-107) — reach_threat, approach_displace, close_rate, stophit_sigma — takes `longer`/`shorter` as fixed args. Only grip_position is recomputed per beat (wrapper.py:66 via grip_target). D3 says 'the wrapper's frozen-at-engagement-start er dict recomputes per-beat' but reach_sigma in the CLOSED exchange (wrapper.py:141) also consumes the frozen `er` dict, and the reopen logic (wrapper.py:78-84) recomputes base_gap from the frozen er[longer]-er[shorter]. If reach becomes grip-aware and the wrapper only refreshes er[c] but not the longer/shorter assignment and measure_gap derivation, a beat where the choked longer weapon now reads SHORTER than its opponent will still be labeled `longer` and still drive reopen/stop-hit as the long weapon.
findings.recommendation: Scope D3 to refresh the FULL measure state per-beat (er, then re-derive longer/shorter/measure_gap from the refreshed er), not just er[c]. Add an explicit invariant test: after per-beat er refresh, longer==argmax(er) every beat. Decide deliberately whether the longer/shorter LABEL is allowed to flip mid-engagement (it drives reopen_moment and the whole approach asymmetry) — that is a design fork, not a mechanical refresh.

findings.claim: D8/D9's 'wrapper branches mirror how bind/riposte/neutralize already work' does NOT hold structurally. Bind is a nested 3-iteration inner loop that MUTATES beats and can return-felled mid-loop (wrapper.py:270-279); riposte is a role-swap tail (wrapper.py:280-288). A grab/pin/contact phase with a BRANCHING four-outcome menu (disarm/throw/pin/control/seize/foot-pin/escape) has no analogous single insertion point — the state_graph's declared contact.axis injection point is pinned to node 'Bind' (state_graph.py:88-90) but the plan's D9 gates grabs on 'bind / beaten-aside / deep commit', which in the code are THREE different, non-adjacent sites (bind at wrapper.py:260, beat_aside/slip_inside displace at wrapper.py:229-240, deep-commit reopen at wrapper.py:244). Inserting one contact phase that all three feed requires restructuring the outcome tail, not mirroring one branch.
findings.evidence: state_graph.py INJECTION_POINTS['contact.axis'] node='Bind', site='(WS-5, unbuilt)'. But the plan's grab preconditions map to: (1) bind — the `if bind:` block wrapper.py:260-279 (a self-contained inner loop); (2) beaten-aside — the DISPLACE-AND-STEP-INSIDE block wrapper.py:220-240 (gated on aggressor.w['head']=='point', which per finding-1 doesn't read sel_head so composites with point-elements never trigger it — plan's own D5 note); (3) deep commit — reopen_moment set at wrapper.py:244. These three are interleaved with the hit/riposte/bind outcome mapping (wrapper.py:187-219) and the reopen-moment bookkeeping (wrapper.py:241-252). The state graph has no Contact node in STATES (state_graph.py:30-51) — only the injection-point METADATA references it, and its `to`/`emits`/terminal wiring is absent, so 'contact.axis goes live' is not a drop-in.
findings.recommendation: Add a real Contact STATE to state_graph.STATES with explicit to/emits edges BEFORE wiring contact.py, and choose ONE insertion point in the outcome tail (after the hit/bind/riposte mapping resolves, wrapper.py:~253) that reads a unified 'opening_created' flag set by all three precondition sites — rather than three parallel grab checks. Prove the branching menu terminates without re-entering the bind inner loop's beats-mutation. The 'mirrors bind/riposte' claim should be dropped; it mirrors NEITHER cleanly.

findings.claim: The bake-once cache (_derived) is the load-bearing coupling between the static-guard world and the proposed dynamic world, and the plan does not address it. hand_guard/blade_guard/gap/geo/_derived are ALL baked once at import and STORED ON THE WEAPON DICT (weapons.py:735-766). Because WEAPONS records are SHARED singletons (combatant.w returns WEAPONS[self.weapon], combatant.py:122), any per-beat circumstance state MUST live on the Combatant, never on the weapon dict — but derive()/at_grip()/percussion_authority all take only `w`, so a circumstance-aware derivation cannot be memoized the way _derived is, and naively re-deriving per beat silently defeats the bake-once optimization the roster depends on (derive() is called via agility/at_grip/defense_affinities/percussion_authority many times per beat — the exact reason the cache exists, weapons.py:749-750).
findings.evidence: weapons.py:756 bakes _rec['_derived']=_wp.derive(_rec); derive() (weapon_physics.py:146-148) returns the cache when present and its docstring warns 'mutation after bake is out of contract.' at_grip (weapon_physics.py:462-463) reads d=derive(w) (cached PoB/MoI) then recomputes I_g fresh per call — so at_grip is ALREADY the model for 'static base + per-circumstance recompute' and is NOT cached. But the plan's D1 at_circumstance ADDS omega_cap/m_eff/rear_clearance and D2 routes heft/percussion through it: these become per-beat recomputes with no cache, called from core.damage (per hit) and select_mode (per candidate head per beat). hand_guard/blade_guard stay baked static scalars on the dict (weapons.py:764-766) read by mode_sigma (systems.py:188,193), bind_sigma (systems.py:473), defense_affinities (weapon_physics.py:281,287) — so 'guard quantities' remain frozen while 'reach/damage quantities' go live, the exact static/dynamic split the prompt names.
findings.recommendation: Explicitly rule that circumstance-aware quantities (omega_cap, m_eff, heft-under-grip, percussion-under-grip) take (w, circumstance-scalars) and are NEVER written back onto the shared WEAPONS dict; keep _derived as the static base they build on. Document that guards remain static BY DESIGN (a cross-guard's catch area doesn't change with grip) and audit that no consumer mixes a static-guard-derived defense affinity with a dynamic-reach-derived threat in a way that double-reads the same geometry inconsistently. Add a per-beat memo keyed on (weapon, round(grip,k), round(range_avail,k)) on the Combatant if profiling shows the uncached re-derive is hot.

findings.claim: core.strike routes sel_head (dynamic) into damage() but pairs it with whole-weapon w['gap'] and whole-weapon percussion_authority — a PRE-EXISTING half-baked mode that D2/D5 will amplify rather than introduce. The plan should fix it in the same pass, not inherit it.
findings.evidence: core.strike (core.py:169-171): head=sel_head (per-beat selected), but gap_prec=attacker.w['gap'] and perc=WP.percussion_authority(attacker.w) are both whole-weapon. So a bec_de_corbin selecting its top-spike (geo point_concentration 0.88) is damaged with the whole-weapon baked gap (from geometry point_concentration 0.86 — coincidentally close here, but for goedendag the whole-weapon pc is 0.25 while its spike mode_element pc is 0.75, a large mismatch). adef_cap (systems.py:219-220) has the same issue: it reads w['gap'] for the point/cut_thrust path regardless of which element's point was selected. select_mode's own comparator (systems.py:326) also uses w['gap'] uniformly.
findings.recommendation: When D5 threads per-element geo into select_mode, thread the SELECTED element's baked geo['gap'] onto the Combatant (a sel_gap field beside sel_head/sel_dmg) and read it in core.strike/adef_cap instead of w['gap']. This closes the existing whole-weapon/per-element gap for composites in the same increment that widens the mode system, rather than leaving I4 with a known per-element/whole-weapon inconsistency.

findings.claim: D9's hook_affordance 'saturating sum over dual_role_element guards' will double-read geometry that the mass model and blade_guard already consume, risking a triple-count unless scoped. dual_role_element guards carry mass_kg=0 (counted via elements) but their extent_m IS already summed into blade_guard via _guard_catch_raw.
findings.evidence: weapon_physics._guard_catch_raw (weapon_physics.py:316-324) sums g['extent_m'] over ALL guards including dual_role_element ones (it does not skip them — only _all_parts at weapon_physics.py:126 skips dual_role for MASS). So blade_guard (weapon_physics.py:333-338) already credits the guisarme hook / dangpa tines / partisan wing-lugs as catch surface. A new hook_affordance(w) 'saturating sum over dual_role_element guards' (plan D9) would re-sum the SAME extents that blade_guard already saturates, and those same elements also appear in the mass model's elements[] list. Three readers of one physical extent.
findings.recommendation: Scope hook_affordance to the GRAB/PIN axis only (contact.py) and prove it is orthogonal to blade_guard's bind-catch (grab = seize/control via hardware, bind-catch = deflect an opposing blade) — or derive grab affinity as a documented FUNCTION of blade_guard rather than a fresh independent sum over the same guards, so the two are not silently additive on the same extent_m.
